engineering.translation: With self unattached to external contacts (bāhya-sparśeṣu asaktātmā), one finds happiness within the self (ātmani sukham). With the self yoked to brahman through brahma-yoga, one obtains imperishable bliss (akṣayam sukham). The verse establishes that the bliss-source is internal — happiness in the self — accessed by detachment from external contacts. Once yoked to brahman through brahma-yoga, the bliss is imperishable. The engineering analog: the senior whose satisfaction comes from the work itself — the quiet of a clean migration, the readability of a well-designed system, the engagement of a difficult problem rightly approached — has access to a non-bounded source. The praise from a manager, the launch's reception, the conference invitation are bounded contacts. STRETCHED honestly because brahma-yoga and akṣaya-sukha exceed; the operational shadow is preserved.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A principal engineer has, across a decade, audited where her satisfaction actually comes from. She tracks: the quiet of the Tuesday-afternoon design session in which a hard architectural decision yielded; the readability of the Postgres-schema document she wrote that her successor still uses two years later; the moment in the standup when the new hire's correction landed and shifted her own thinking; the fifteen minutes after the migration shipped clean when she sat watching the Datadog latency curve hold. None of these were the conference talk, the launch tweet, the executive recognition. The internal-bliss source is operational: it sits inside the work itself, not in the work's reception. When the conference invitation arrives, she accepts or declines on the merits; the internal-bliss source is not affected. STRETCHED honestly; the brahma-yoga akṣaya-sukha reach further than the operational satisfaction-from-the-work.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader hears 'happiness within' as 'be intrinsically motivated' coaching content. The verse names a structural fact about where the bliss-source sits — inside, not in external contact. This is not a motivational hack; it is an audit-result.
engineering.counter_example: When an engineer claims internal-bliss while the satisfaction is in fact externally sourced — when the audit would reveal that praise, recognition, and external reception drive the affect — the verse does not apply. The internal source is testable by the audit; claiming it without doing the audit is performative.
engineering.implication: Audit, across the last quarter, where your satisfaction actually came from. Internal sources versus external contacts. The verse names the imperishable as internally sourced.
engineering.quotable_line: Satisfaction sources audited honestly. The internal sits inside the work; the external is bounded by contact.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 5.21 engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored to chapter-5 thesis: equivalence-of-paths-with-karma-yoga-preference, decoupled identity, sama-darśana, internal-bliss-source, brahma-nirvāṇa-as-operational-rung-with-honest-STRETCHED. Cross-references to chapters 2-4 preserved.

v10: re-tagged STRETCHED=False, confidence=HIGH per chapter-5 thesis review (the verse's operational analog is fully honored without stretching the verse's metaphysical scope past where the engineering layer can carry).
